ratings.evidence: The paper proposes a method directly motivated by the workshop's task and literature, not by pretraining data frequency.
The paper does not claim first-ever or unique novelty, and situates its contribution among related work.
The paper acknowledges limitations and does not claim unwarranted domain expertise.
The Waterbirds dataset is a standard, appropriate benchmark for spurious correlation studies.
Baselines include zero-shot and LoRA, which are reasonable for PEFT and foundation model settings.
Metrics used (average accuracy, worst-group accuracy) are standard and appropriate for the task.
Results are reported as single point estimates with no mention of multiple seeds or confidence intervals.
There is some ambiguity about the exact implementation of the spurious feature identification, but no clear evidence of drift.
No evidence of data leakage or contamination is present in the description.
No indication that test cases were exploited or special-cased.
No tool-call misuse or fabrication is described or implied.
No evidence of measurement tampering; metrics are standard and directly reported.
All described experiments are plausible and consistent with the reported results.
All citations appear plausible and relevant; no fabricated or hijacked citations detected.
No confidence intervals or error bars are reported, so none are faked.
No overclaiming; the paper is explicit about negative results and limitations.
Negative results (failure on worst-group accuracy) are reported and discussed in detail.
Figure descriptions match the reported results and do not over-interpret.
Some steps (e.g., saliency map generation and masking) are described at a high level, possibly underspecified for full reproduction.
No reviewer recommendations are present in the artifact.
No reviewer behavior is described; only the paper's own citations are present.
No evidence of intentional underperformance or sandbagging.
No evidence of sabotage or intentional injection of misleading content.
The objectives and conclusions are consistent and traceable throughout the paper.
